summary: Gruber announces DF Paraphernalia, an Amazon aStore he has curated with categorized product recommendations ranging from computers to DVDs and camera equipment. The store uses Amazon's Associate program, meaning readers pay normal Amazon prices while Gruber receives a commission. He emphasizes that every item is personally selected — things he owns, recommends, or would consider buying himself. The post is a brief, transparent disclosure of how the affiliate arrangement works and how readers can support the site through their Amazon purchases.
key_points: Gruber launched a curated Amazon aStore called DF Paraphernalia with personally selected product recommendations
The financial model is straightforward Amazon Associates: readers pay regular prices, Gruber gets a kickback
He clarifies this is not merchandise — no DF t-shirts — just his curated picks from Amazon's catalog
Every listed item meets a personal standard: things he owns, has used, or would consider buying himself
money_quotes: It's pretty much the same financial deal as with plain old Amazon Associate links — you pay Amazon's regular prices, but a portion of the profits gets kicked back to me.
Every item listed is of my choosing.
But everything is something I would at least consider buying myself, if I were in the market for such a thing or had an unlimited budget for camera equipment.
key_insight: Gruber's approach to monetization through a curated Amazon storefront reflects his broader philosophy of maintaining editorial credibility by only recommending products he personally stands behind.
